behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is cross-module wiring between evaluate-stage logic and artifact persistence keys. A unit test of a single function can miss real key-routing mistakes in command-to-store integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run the evaluate-stage command end-to-end against a real local artifact store (temp filesystem or local DB). Assert that `grounding` is created/updated and `plan` is not written by this stage.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This depends on read/write behavior across implement flow + artifact storage contracts. Mocking either side can hide real regressions where grounding is ignored or output is written to the wrong slot.
behaviors.test_description: Seed artifact storage with a `grounding` artifact, run implement-stage flow, then verify: grounding is read/used (e.g., appears in assembled implementation context or trace) and output is written to `plan` while `grounding` remains intact.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: The risk is conditional behavior across issue-content parsing plus artifact write side effects. A pure unit mock can miss accidental write calls that happen only in integrated flow wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content that already contains diagnosis, run deep-dive stage with real local artifact persistence, and assert no new write/update to `plan` occurs from deep-dive.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a multi-step pipeline property across stages. Real failure appears in handoff sequencing and agent-step interactions; isolated stage tests can pass while end-to-end propagation fails.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full chain in sequence with real stage transitions and artifact persistence. Assert that grounding generated in evaluate is available downstream and materially reflected in final `plan` content/structure at implement output.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: The important failure is fallback behavior plus non-corruption of neighboring artifacts in real storage state transitions. In-process mocks may not catch unintended overwrite/deletion side effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement-stage with no `grounding` artifact in a real local artifact store that contains other artifacts. Verify `plan` is emitted, fallback path succeeds, and pre-existing artifacts are unchanged.
